title: RNA/DNA Extraction from Acute Gastroenteritis Samples
author: Hengyun Guan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.srjed4n

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2g stool to 1.0ml phosphate buffer saline (with Mg2+ and Ca2+) in one 1.5ml Eppendorf tube with 6-8 particles of ceramic beads. Put the anal swab into 1.0ml phosphate buffer saline (with Mg 2+ and Ca 2+ ) and repeatedly squeeze it. Vortex for 2×20 sec at 4000rpm at room temperature. Centrifuge at 8000 rpm for 10 min at 4℃. Collect the supernatant.
5. Throat swabs or environmental surface swabs were stored in 3.5ml Hank’s solution （Yocon Catalog #:MT0301-1）. 6. Vortex for 40 sec at 4000rpm at room temperature.
7. Add 15ml contaminated water to centrifugal filter (Merck Millipore Ltd., Ireland). 8. Centrifuge at 8000rpm for 5min at 4℃. 9. Repeat step 8 for three times. 10. Collect the supernatant.

16. Add each above 200ul supernatant in sample cartridge to extract RNA/DNA followed the manufacturer’s instructions (MagNA Pure LC Total Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit, Roche, Germany). 17. RNA/DNA was suspended in 50ul of elution buffer. 18. RNA/DNA was amplified immediately or stored at −80℃.

steps.title: Select randomly six points on the...
steps.instruction: Select randomly six points on the surface of food specimen, then cut them into 2mm×2mm×2mm pieces by sterilized scissors. Add 0.2g food pieces to 1.0ml phosphate buffer saline (with Mg 2+ and Ca 2+ ) in one 1.5ml Eppendorf tube with 6-8 particles of ceramic beads. Vortex for 2×20 sec at 4000rpm at room temperature. Centrifuge at 8000 rpm for 10 min at 4℃. Collect the supernatant.

steps.title: The left supernatant was stored at...
steps.instruction: The left supernatant was stored at −80℃ after the RNA/DNA extraction.
